architectureDecisions: Scout exports only current relational records it actually stores today; it does not claim canonical relationship-set, attribution-path, outcome-event, vector, LanceDB, or pgvector persistence.
The tenant slug is the initial layer mapping for exported anchors; tenant ID/slug mismatches fail closed.
Dry run validates and reports without returning records, writing Scout data, calling Enterprise/Fortress, or calling Cloud.
The Enterprise/Fortress importer must be supplied by the private runtime or a reviewed local private tool; Scout/open-core must not fake it.
Enterprise/Fortress owns the 2026-06-18.scout-lancedb-import.v1 contract in ucl-vector.
Scout does not need to export embeddings; Enterprise/Fortress generates embeddings locally before LanceDB import records are valid.
LanceDB import records require dense finite local embeddings, tenant/layer consistency, citation/provenance coverage, safe metadata, and idempotency keys.

Cloud must not receive raw customer data, exact data items, vectors, embeddings, relationship sets, attribution paths, outcome events, prompts, generated content, evidence packs, migration logs, checkpoints, failed payloads, or customer-specific derived intelligence.
Cloud upgrade/onboarding state is currently derived from existing commercial/control-plane metadata until a dedicated persisted onboarding entity or read model is implemented.

Cloud entitlement success authorises access to paid local capability only; local Scout/Fortress preflight, backup, storage migration, LanceDB/pgvector proof, vector backfill, relationship indexing, and governed JSON verification remain customer data-plane work.
End-to-end upgrade proof remains partial until the Scout operator export CLI, private Enterprise/Fortress importer, and quick deterministic local API smoke exist.
